title: Otsuka & Toshima Sightseeing Guide: Trams, Gardens, and Sugamo's Old Tokyo Charm
excerpt: A guide to the tram lines, gardens, and shopping streets around Otsuka, and where to stay nearby.
contentHtml:
Otsuka's biggest draw is its unhurried, old-fashioned atmosphere, anchored by one of Tokyo's last surviving tram lines and a scattering of traditional gardens nearby.

Top Sights

The Toden Arakawa Line (Sakura Tram) — A slow, scenic tram that runs right through the neighborhood.

Sugamo Jizo-dori Shopping Street — A shopping street beloved by older generations, with an old-Tokyo feel.

Ikebukuro Sunshine City — A large complex with shopping, an aquarium, and observation deck, a short trip away.

Kyu-Furukawa Gardens — A Western-style mansion and traditional Japanese garden combined.

Rikugien — One of Tokyo's most celebrated traditional strolling gardens.

Food

Sugamo's old-fashioned shops sell traditional sweets and snacks, while Ikebukuro nearby offers an enormous, multicultural range of restaurants, and Otsuka itself has a handful of local izakaya worth seeking out.

Where to Stay

For an easy, low-key base near it all, Plat House Otsuka is within walking distance of Otsuka Station, with a full kitchen, Netflix, and Nintendo Switch. Direct booking beats OTA pricing, with free cancellation up to 7 days out.
